intro_script: Welcome back! It's June 29th, 2026. Today in robotics news: updates from RoboCup, advancements in industrial automation, and a look ahead at AI integration. Tune in for your daily dose of tech intelligence.
outro_script: That's your RoboDigest for June 29th, 2026! Hope you're excited for RoboCup! Keep those circuits buzzing, and thanks for tuning in. See you tomorrow!

items.title: What’s coming up at #RoboCup2026?
items.summary: RoboCup 2026 is scheduled to take place in Incheon, South Korea, from July 2nd to the 6th. The event encompasses competitions, training sessions, and a symposium for participants. Updates are anticipated regarding both the leagues and the overall competition format. A major focus this year will be placed specifically on the soccer leagues.
items.audio_script: From Robohub, "What’s coming up at #RoboCup2026?". The coverage details the upcoming RoboCup 2026 event in Incheon, South Korea, scheduled from July second to the sixth. This gathering is slated to include competitive demonstrations, dedicated training modules, and an academic symposium for all registered participants. Updates are expected regarding both the structure of various leagues and the overall competition methodology employed across the duration. A significant area of focus for this iteration will be centered specifically on the technical aspects of the soccer league competitions. The event represents a key benchmark for advancements in autonomous robotics within the field, setting expectations for further development announcements shortly.

items.title: AO-ARC: Almost-Surely Asymptotically Optimal Multi-Robot Motion Planning with ARC
items.summary: AO-ARC is introduced as an anytime multi-robot motion planning (MRMP) method. It achieves initial solution times comparable to existing state-of-the-art feasibility solvers. Crucially, AO-ARC converges faster and more reliably than current anytime MRMP methods as the robot count increases. The technique adapts the AO-x meta-algorithm by iteratively calling ARC on bounded MRMP instances using a makespan cost metric. Theoretical analysis confirms its asymptotic optimality, which was validated through 2D and 3D empirical tests.

items.title: Spacecraft Fiducial Marker for Autonomous Rendezvous, Proximity Operations, and Docking
items.summary: Autonomous robotic operations in space require reliable visual references for rendezvous and docking due to environmental challenges. Current single-scale fiducial markers fail during critical close-range proximity phases. This paper introduces AstraTag, a novel marker utilizing a square Spidron pattern with recursive self-similarity for multi-scale detection. Identification employs a 48-bit signature encoded with a GRS code and includes contour-based localization. Critically, AstraTag incorporates a Thin-Plate Spline re-warp fallback to ensure robust performance on curved spacecraft surfaces compared to existing benchmarks.

items.title: SceneBot: Contact-Prompted General Humanoid Whole Body Tracking with Scene-Interaction
items.summary: SceneBot is a new motion-tracking framework designed to overcome limitations in current humanoid reinforcement learning when performing contact-rich tasks. It unifies control for free-space locomotion, terrain traversal, and whole-body manipulation by conditioning a single policy on reference motions and per-link contact labels. To handle data scarcity, the authors propose a hindsight scene reconstruction method that infers interaction graphs from human motion. Trained on 7.5 hours of this reconstructed data, SceneBot generalizes effectively to novel environments and movements. The framework demonstrates the ability to seamlessly execute complex tasks requiring both free movement and object interaction.

items.title: Learning to Throw: Agile and Accurate Cable-Suspended Payload Delivery with a Quadrotor
items.summary: Quadrotors are valuable for time-critical transport like search-and-rescue missions, but precisely releasing suspended payloads remains an underexplored area. Existing model-based methods struggle with rope flexibility and tracking errors due to overly conservative constraints. Researchers propose a novel hybrid simulation framework coupling a high-fidelity quadrotor model with a physics solver for accurate rope-payload force exchange. A deep reinforcement learning policy trained in this environment achieves agile, accurate payload throws, outperforming baselines by reducing landing error up to 50%. Furthermore, the system can be trained using only visual observations, demonstrating comparable accuracy to state estimation methods.

items.title: Physics-Guided Robotic Radiation Source Localization along Arbitrary Measurement Paths in Unstructured Environments
items.summary: Developing reliable techniques for locating radiation sources using robots is crucial for enhancing safety and efficiency. Existing approaches often require robots to approach the source, which poses risks of radiation damage. This work introduces an automation framework utilizing a physics-informed machine learning (PIML) model for precise source localization along any measurement path in unknown environments. The method incorporates physics-inspired tensors to manage attenuated gamma-ray flux signals from various obstacles while computing multiple models concurrently for improved robustness. Validation was performed through high-fidelity simulations and physical experiments, advancing robot radiation perception toward spatial intelligence.

items.title: P-ARC: Exploiting Subproblem Independence for Parallel Multi-Robot Motion Planning
items.summary: Presents Parallel ARC (P-ARC), a parallel extension of the Adaptive Robot Coordination (ARC) method for multi-robot motion planning (MRMP). P-ARC achieves parallelism across the three core stages of ARC: initial solutions, conflict detection, and conflict resolution by exploiting problem independence. A hybrid strategy, OR-P-ARC, is introduced using an OR-parallel multi-start approach with both ARC and P-ARC. Evaluations utilized 2D mobile and planar manipulator scenarios involving up to 128 robots to test parallel efficiency. The research demonstrates planning time speedups approaching four times over the sequential version for large Panda multi-manipulator teams on 16 CPU cores.

items.title: Direct Action-Head Injection of A Grounded 3D Point Unlocks Spatial and Task Generalization
items.summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models struggle with spatial and task changes when deployed. Previous methods relying on language or visual prompting fail to solve this brittleness effectively. The research proposes a novel, model-agnostic module that represents grounding signals using 3D points. This 3D embedding is computed relative to the gripper and injected directly into the action head via adaptive layer normalization. Implementation on the LIBERO-PRO benchmark significantly boosted success rates for both task and position perturbations, confirming that 3D injection into the action head is key to generalization.

items.title: CWI: Composite Humanoid Whole-Body Imitation System for Loco-manipulation
items.summary: Developed Composite Whole-Body Imitation (CWI) to address challenges in coordinating stable locomotion and versatile manipulation for humanoid robots. CWI decouples upper-body manipulation data from lower-body locomotion control, allowing the use of diverse MoCap datasets while ensuring stable movement. Lower-body dynamics are maintained using an Adversarial Motion Prior guided by dual discriminators trained on curated walking and squatting clips. The framework employs a multi-critic architecture to balance conflicting objectives among motion style, locomotion, and manipulation. Testing on simulation and a physical LimX Oli robot demonstrated competitive performance with robust whole-body coordination from minimal input commands.

items.title: DIM-WAM: World-Action Modeling with Diverse Historical Event Memory
items.summary: World-action models often struggle with long-horizon tasks because they rely too heavily on short-term history. DiM-WAM addresses this by introducing a memory-augmented framework to incorporate multi-scale historical context, future dynamics, and global task progress. The model extracts compact visual event information into multiple merging memory banks. It uses these banked memories to condition video and action denoising for better long-term awareness. Evaluation on RMBench showed DiM-WAM boosting average success from 28.4% to 69.8%, significantly outperforming existing baselines on both simulated and real-world tasks.
